briefdescription.para: edges_: EdgeId -> edge data
edgePerVertex_: VertId -> one edge id of one of edges with origin there
each true bit here corresponds to valid element in edgePerVertex_
the number of valid elements in edgePerVertex_ or set bits in validVerts_

detaileddescription.para: builds this topology from given contours
also builds the vector of referenced points using two functors: reserve and add if all even edges are consistently oriented, then the output contours will be oriented the same

inbodydescription.para: ignore contours with 0 or 1 points because of no edges in them
ignore contours with 0 or 1 points because of no edges in them

detaileddescription.para:
build topology of comp2firstVert.size()-1 not-closed polylines each pair (a,b) of indices in
comp2firstVert
defines vertex range of a polyline [a,b)

briefdescription.para: converts this topology into contours of given type using the functor returning point by its Id
creates an edge not associated with any vertex

detaileddescription.para:
makes an edge connecting vertices a and b
e
if valid then the function does not make new edge, but connects the vertices using given one (and returns it)
if 1) any of the vertices is invalid or not within the vertSize(), 2) or a==b, 3) or either a or b already has 2 incident edges, 4) given edge e is not lone or not within the edgeSize() then makeEdge(a,b) does nothing and returns invalid edge

for every given edge[ue] calls makeEdge( edge[ue][0], edge[ue][1], ue ), making new edges so that edges.size() <= undirectedEdgeSize() at the end
the total number of edges created

briefdescription.para: checks whether the edge is disconnected from all other edges and disassociated from all vertices (as if after makeEdge)
returns last not lone undirected edge id, or invalid id if no such edge exists
returns last not lone edge id, or invalid id if no such edge exists
returns the number of half-edge records including lone ones
returns the number of allocated edge records
returns the number of undirected edges (pairs of half-edges) including lone ones
returns the number of allocated undirected edges (pairs of half-edges)
computes the number of not-lone (valid) undirected edges
sets the capacity of half-edges vector
returns true if given edge is within valid range and not-lone
given edge becomes lone after the call, so it is un-spliced from connected edges, and if it was not connected at origin or destination, then that vertex is deleted as well
calls deleteEdge for every set bit
returns the amount of memory this object occupies on heap

detaileddescription.para: the cut in rings in both cases is made after a and b

briefdescription.para: next (counter clock wise) half-edge in the origin ring
returns origin vertex of half-edge
returns destination vertex of half-edge

detaileddescription.para: sets new origin to the full origin ring including this edge

briefdescription.para: for all valid vertices this vector contains an edge with the origin there
for all edges this vector contains its origin
returns valid edge if given vertex is present in the mesh
returns true if given vertex is present in the mesh
returns 0 if given vertex does not exist, 1 if it has one incident edge, and 2 if it has two incident edges
returns the number of valid vertices
returns last valid vertex id, or invalid id if no single valid vertex exists
creates new vert-id not associated with any edge yet
explicitly increases the size of vertices vector
explicitly increases the size of vertices vector, doubling the current capacity if it was not enough
sets the capacity of vertices vector
returns the number of vertex records including invalid ones
returns the number of allocated vert records
returns cached set of all valid vertices
if region pointer is not null then converts it in reference, otherwise returns all valid vertices in the mesh
finds and returns edge from o to d in the mesh; returns invalid edge otherwise
returns all vertices incident to path edges

detaileddescription.para: split given edge on two parts: dest(returned-edge) = org(e) - newly created vertex, org(returned-edge) = org(e-before-split), dest(e) = dest(e-before-split)
adds polyline in this topology passing progressively via vertices *[vs, vs+num); if vs[0] == vs[num-1] then a closed polyline is created; return the edge from first to second vertex

briefdescription.para: appends polyline topology (from) in addition to the current topology: creates new edges, verts;
saves this in binary stream
loads this from binary stream
comparison via edges (all other members are considered as not important caches)
returns true if for each edge e: e == e.next() || e.odd() == next( e ).sym().odd()
changes the orientation of all edges: every edge e is replaced with e.sym()
verifies that all internal data structures are valid
computes numValidVerts_ and validVerts_ from edgePerVertex_
returns true if the polyline does not have any holes
sets new origin to the full origin ring including this edge, without updating edgePerVertex_ table

detaileddescription.para: topology of one or several polylines (how line segments are connected in lines) common for 2D and 3D polylines
